creator: truckman@FreeBSD.org
text:
If I start OpenOffice writer (built with the CUPS option, which is the default):

  Format -> Page -> Paper Format: DL Envelope
                    Orientation: Landscape
                    Margins: about 1/4"

The envelope can be edited in a user-friendly orientation.  I added the return and destination addresses.

I don't have a DL envelope, so I loaded a sheet of letter size (8.5 x 11") paper into the printer, with the outline of a #10 (4 1/8 x 9 1/2") envelope fed narrow side first traced on it.  I then printed my document to my Epson printer, with the Paper Properties set to:
  Paper Size: DL
  Orientation: Landscape

The preview shows the text correctly positioned within the printable borders.

If I had drawn a DL-size outline, the return address would have been closer to the upper left corner.
